transcript:
Sarah (Project Manager): Thanks everyone for joining. Let’s start with the progress update on the internal automation tool.

James (Developer): Over the past week, we completed the core API endpoints. The system can now process requests and store results in our internal database. However, we still need to refine error handling and logging.

Sarah: That sounds good. What about the integration with the email system?

James: The integration is partially done. We can generate draft emails automatically based on meeting summaries, but we haven’t implemented the actual sending function yet.

Linda (Operations): From an operations standpoint, automation will save us quite a bit of time, especially for follow-up emails after meetings.

Sarah: Great. For the next sprint, let’s focus on improving reliability and testing the email generation feature. We should also prepare a simple user interface so non-technical team members can use the tool.

James: Understood. I’ll prioritize those tasks this week.

Sarah: Perfect. Let’s regroup next Monday to review progress.
meeting_summary: The team discussed the progress on the internal automation tool, highlighting the completion of core API endpoints and partial integration with the email system. The focus for the next sprint will be on improving the email feature and preparing a user interface.
key_decisions: Focus on improving reliability and testing the email generation feature in the next sprint.
Prepare a simple user interface for non-technical team members.

action_items.task: Refine error handling and logging for the API.
Complete the implementation of the actual sending function for draft emails.
Prepare a user interface for the automation tool.

risks: Reliability issues with the email generation feature.
Incomplete user interface may hinder non-technical team members from using the tool.
